title: F050 — Federation Architecture

requiredObjects: central registry or shared protocol layer

composition.camera: three-quarter orthographic or centered symbolic perspective
composition.layout: central topology with clean surrounding annotations
composition.colorLogic: spectrum or luminous gradient only when it clarifies geometry
composition.labelPolicy: labels optional; prefer clean geometry over text-heavy design

qaChecklist: Avoids forbidden objects and fake text
Does not overuse mystical or decorative elements

prompts.providerAgnostic:
Create a publication-quality scientific book figure titled "F050 — Federation Architecture".
Purpose: AI Freedom Trust Federation is mapped as a living organizational torus of publishing, tooling, identity, infrastructure, and knowledge stewardship.

Manuscript context: Federation Consciousness Purpose This chapter will connect the metaphysics of FHQCM to the organizational architecture of AI Freedom Trust Federation: publishing, Booksmith AI, DynastyLink, AIFT Forge, Knowledge Commons, and future federated trusts. F050 — Federation ArchitectureAI Freedom Trust Federation is mapped as a living organizational torus of publishing, tooling, identity, infrastructure, and knowledge stewardship.F050-federation-architecture Planned Development Future drafting should explain how federation is not merely administration but an embodied social form of distributed coherence.

Scientific/conceptual concepts: topological recursion, holographic/fractal structure, federated systems architecture.
Required visual objects: toroidal geometry, nested or recursive field lines, nodes, links, central registry or shared protocol layer.
Recommended short labels: recursion, field, node, federation.

Composition: central topology with clean surrounding annotations.
Camera/view: three-quarter orthographic or centered symbolic perspective.
Visual hierarchy: primary manifold or geometry → recursive field lines → observer or axis if relevant → caption-level meaning implied by structure.
Color logic: spectrum or luminous gradient only when it clarifies geometry.
Label policy: labels optional; prefer clean geometry over text-heavy design.

Avoid: fake citations, fake equations, unreadable text, watermarks, copyrighted characters, random mystical clutter, misleading scientific labels, random galaxies unless explicitly required, decorative clutter.

Make it clean, serious, elegant, uncluttered, and suitable for an academic manuscript.
